rationale: Verbal-tier guides (2025, 2026) map directly: Legendary Performer and Elite Producer collapse to Elite (Legendary Performer is a 2026-only super-elite designation atop Elite Producer); Weekly Starter -> Starter; Flex Play -> Flex; Benchwarmer / Waiver Wire / Waiver Wire Add -> Depth (all three describe roster-edge holds); Dart Throw -> Dart Throw. The 2026 'Waiver Wire' and 'Waiver Wire Add' labels are treated as equivalent. For numeric guides, proportional bucketing matched the user's defaults. 15-tier schemes (2022 numeric strings, 2023 'Tier N'): Tier 1 -> Elite (~7%); Tiers 2-3 -> Starter (~13%); Tiers 4-6 -> Flex (~20%); Tiers 7-9 -> Depth (~20%); Tiers 10-15 -> Dart Throw (~40%). 12-tier scheme (2024): Tier 1 -> Elite; Tiers 2-3 -> Starter; Tiers 4-5 -> Flex; Tiers 6-8 -> Depth; Tiers 9-12 -> Dart Throw. Synthetic Dart Throws: 2022 'Z-Prospect Ranked' (player appeared in Z-Prospect Cheatsheet but not Post-Draft Rankings) and 2024 'Unranked' (profiled but not in Top-48) are both treated as Dart Throws because the author chose not to rank them in the main hierarchy. The 2025 file unexpectedly contains five QB entries (Cam Ward, Shedeur Sanders, Jaxson Dart, Jalen Milroe, Tyler Shough) tagged with numeric 'Tier 7/9/10/11' labels rather than the ZAP verbal tiers; these were mapped using the 2024 12-tier convention (Tier 7-8 -> Depth, Tier 9+ -> Dart Throw) because the QB cheatsheet in 2025 uses a similar 12-tier post-draft ranking structure.
